tender.title: RFP No NOR26-103 Interior Flooring and Leveling

unspscLevelData.unspscLevel1: Building and Construction and Maintenance Services
Structures and Building and Construction and Manufacturing Components and Supplies

procumentEntityData.name: Nova Scotia Provincial Housing Agency
procumentEntityData.nameFr: Nova Scotia Provincial Housing Agency FR

tender.description:
The NSPHA is seeking for prospective Proponents for leveling floors of the main corridor and units at 22 Junction Rd, Springhill, NS B0M 1X0.
The Hilltop Manor floor levelling work will be completed in an actively occupied seniors’ residential building, with an estimated 60% occupancy throughout construction. (Anticipated Upper Floor occupancy at initial stage). At each phase, one floor will be vacated, and the work will be completed before moving to the next level.
The Proponents shall plan and execute the work with full consideration of tenant safety, accessibility, and continuity of operations.

attachments.filename: RFP NOR26-103 Interior Flooring and Leveling-0183082.zip

tender.memo:
Mandatory Site Tour

The mandatory site-tour will be held at the following location and time: Date: June 09, 2026 Time: 1-00 pm Atlantic Time Address: 22 Junction Rd, Springhill, NS B0M 1X0.

Proponents who do not attend the site meeting will be precluded from submitting a proposal.
tender.closingLocation: 3770 Kempt Road, Halifax, Nova Scotia, B3K 4X8

tender.title: Stormwater Management Project (SWMP)  Feasibility and Design Study – Mill Brook

unspscLevelData.unspscLevel1: Management and Business Professionals and Administrative Services
unspscLevelData.unspscLevel4: Feasibility studies or screening of project ideas

tender.description:
The work covered under this RFP is for the completion of the “Stormwater Management Project Feasibility and Design Study - Mill Brook” for the Town of Kentville. The project will include community engagement, technical assessments, hydrologic and hydraulic analysis, environmental review, conceptual infrastructure design, and implementation planning associated with stormwater management and flood mitigation within the Mill Brook watershed area.

This project is intended to provide recommendations based on existing conditions, projected climate impacts, future land use considerations, and evolving infrastructure needs. While previous studies provide valuable foundational information, this project will establish a site-specific framework to address current deficiencies and future climate resilience requirements.

attachments.filename: TOK RFP - Mill Brook Stormwater Feasibility and Design Project Final-0375672.pdf

reasons: matched keywords: stormwater, water, climate, infrastructure, design, construction, RFP

tender.description:
The Atlantic First Nations Water Authority (AFNWA) invites proposals to prequalify a supplier for a Membrane Bioreactor (MBR) system for the Eskasoni First Nation Wastewater Treatment Facility upgrade and expansion.

The selected Proponent will demonstrate relevant experience and capacity to support design development and to supply, deliver, install supervision, and commission an MBR system meeting all project requirements.

procumentEntityData.nameFr: Chignecto-Central Regional Centre for Education FR

tender.contactDetails: To obtain a copy of the tender documents please email tenders@ccrce.ca with the tender title and ID, as well as the name of the business requesting.
tender.description: In general terms, the work will involve but not be limited to the provision of labour, material, equipment and supervision necessary to perform sprinkler work as outlined in the scope of work.
tender.memo:
All persons/companies interested in submitting a tender must attend a mandatory site preview at 10:00 a.m. on Wednesday, June 3, 2026 at 60 Lorne Street, Truro, Nova Scotia 

The CCRCE recommends that interested bidders ensure that proposed subcontractors are in attendance at the mandatory site preview.  Bidders must register their presence with CCRCE stating the name of the contractor they represent.  

Failure to attend and register will lead to the non-acceptance of the tender by CCRCE.
